text:
Arjun Babuta, the Indian shooter taking part in the men’s 10m air rifle final at the Paris Olympics 2024, finished fourth at Chateauroux on Monday. He finished with a total score of 208.4 points after 20 shots in the medal event.

Follow our live updates from the Paris Olympics 2024–HERE

Babuta was off to a strong start in the first two rounds of five shots each where he amassed a total score of 105.1 to place him at third spot and just 0.1 points away from second place.

In a game of minute margins, Babuta could catch up to the leader, Lihao Sheng at the end of the third round but did not capitalise due to a poor fourth round, extending the gap to 1.3 points.

Babuta despite the shaky start to the fifth round, maintained the second spot while the Croatian, Miran Maricic shared the second position with a 21.6 points round. Meanwhile, the leader Sheng was able to extend his lead with a near-perfect shot worth 10.9 points to widen the gap to two points.

READ MORE:India’s Full Schedule at Paris Olympics 2024 for July 29, 2024: Events, Timings, Athletes and More

However, the 25-year-old shooter from Punjab was not able to maintain his position as he dropped down to fourth by the end of the sixth round and was not able to catch up which led to his elimination.

The contest went down to Lihao and the Swede, Victor Lindgren but the world record holder from China was able to take home the gold while Lindgren took the runners up spot and the bronze medal was won by Croatia’s Miran Maricic
